title: Linked List Cycle
prompt: Given the head of a linked list (represented here as `head` array of node values plus `pos`, the index where the tail's next pointer connects, or -1 for none), determine if the list contains a cycle.
constraints: pos is -1 or a valid index in [0, n).
hints: Use Floyd's tortoise and hare: slow advances 1, fast advances 2.
If they ever meet, there is a cycle. If fast reaches null, there isn't.
Hash set of visited nodes also works in O(n) time / O(n) space.
pitfalls: Off-by-one when advancing fast (must check fast && fast.next).
Comparing values instead of node identities.
